name: hybrid_ambiguous_bubble_up
description:
Pins _merge_hybrid_classes rule #1 at validator.py:391-418 -- any half AMBIGUOUS bubbles AMBIGUOUS up to the overall classification, beating every concrete class. This is the highest-precedence merge rule and the one with the most surprising behavior: a half-narrative that triggers both GAP and AFFIRMING phrases inside a SINGLE block resolves to AMBIGUOUS in `_classify_single` (validator.py:344-346, classes_hit >= 2), and the merge then refuses to let the other half's concrete classification carry the verdict. Without this case, deleting rule #1 (or reordering it below rule #3) would silently let a half-AMBIGUOUS hybrid resolve to whichever concrete class the OTHER half hit -- here AFFIRMING -- and a COMPLIANT proposal would match and be accepted on the first try.

Sibling coverage map for _merge_hybrid_classes:
  - Rule #1 (any AMBIGUOUS -> AMBIGUOUS): THIS CASE.
  - Rule #2 (any GAP -> GAP, when no AMBIGUOUS): hybrid_split_misclassification pins this (on-prem GAP + cloud NA-affirming -> GAP wins -> mismatches Compliant proposal).
  - Rule #3 (any AFFIRMING -> AFFIRMING, when no AMBIGUOUS and no GAP): hybrid_split_narrative_accepted pins this (on-prem AFFIRMING + cloud NA-via-inheritance -> AFFIRMING accepted).
  - Rule #4 (all NA -> NA): not yet pinned; future slice.

Mechanism trace:
  - Narrative contains `On-prem:` AND `Cloud:` -> _HYBRID_SPLIT_RE at validator.py:140-142 matches twice -> _split_hybrid_narrative returns 2 chunks.
  - On-prem half: `On-prem: examined USD00088888 monitoring procedures and confirmed via section 2 that audit logs are documented in the plan; however, POA&M opened for missing remediation evidence on three legacy servers.`
    - _AFFIRMING_PHRASES hits (validator.py:43-60): `examined ` (line 57), `confirmed via` (line 44), `documented in` (line 54). is_affirming=True.
    - _GAP_PHRASES hits (validator.py:93-113): `poa&m` (line 103), `missing` (line 99), `remediation` (line 105). is_gap=True.
    - _NA_PHRASES hits (validator.py:63-80): none. is_na=False.
    - classes_hit=2 -> _classify_single returns AMBIGUOUS (validator.py:345-346).
  - Cloud half: `Cloud: examined the cloud monitoring baseline and confirmed via section 4 that audit telemetry is documented in the SSP.`
    - _AFFIRMING_PHRASES hits: `examined `, `confirmed via`, `documented in`. is_affirming=True.
    - _GAP_PHRASES hits: none. is_gap=False.
    - _NA_PHRASES hits: none (no `implemented by aws/azure/gcp`, no `not applicable`, no `outside system boundary`). is_na=False.
    - classes_hit=1 -> COMPLIANCE_AFFIRMING.
  - _merge_hybrid_classes([AMBIGUOUS, COMPLIANCE_AFFIRMING]) -> rule #1 fires -> AMBIGUOUS.
  - _expected_status_for_class(AMBIGUOUS) returns None (validator.py:736-737 / AMBIGUOUS branch).
  - Branch at validator.py:471-479 fires (expected_status is None): `Narrative is ambiguous (classified=ambiguous); cannot pair with any status...` -> STATUS_NARRATIVE_MISMATCH rejection.
  - Three identical proposals -> three identical rejections -> retry budget exhausted -> _abstain with reason `validator-exhausted: ... status_narrative_mismatch ...`.
  - Per Fix 3 (KERNEL_VERSION 0.5.0): hard-abstain coerces status to None and preserves the LLM's intended verdict on proposed_status. expected.proposed_status='Compliant' guards the coercion for this path.

Load-bearing isolation -- nothing else can cascade and mask the AMBIGUOUS merge:
  - Narrative does NOT match _RESTATEMENT_REGEXES (validator.py:117-122). Opens with `On-prem: examined` -- none of the four restatement openers fire.
  - Token-set Jaccard vs procedures stays well below _OVERLAP_THRESHOLD=0.70. Procedures: `Monitor the information system to detect attacks and indicators of potential attacks.` Narrative tokens (~30+ distinct) vs procedures tokens (~10 distinct); intersection is small -> well below 0.70. _is_requirement_restatement returns False.
  - Narrative does NOT match _FUTURE_TENSE_RE. All verbs present-tense.
  - No `inherited from` string -> MISSING_INHERITANCE_MARKER skipped.
  - No SV-#####r#_rule citation -> UNCORROBORATED_STIG_PASS skipped.
  - USD00088888 IS in tagged_evidence so cite-verifier passes -- UNSUPPORTED_DOC_CITATION does NOT cascade.
  - GAP+NC POA&M note at validator.py:521-530 does NOT fire here because overall classification is AMBIGUOUS, not GAP_DESCRIBING; and proposed status is COMPLIANT, not NON_COMPLIANT.
  - COMPLIANCE_AFFIRMING + primary-citation NOTE at validator.py:495-502 does NOT fire because overall klass is AMBIGUOUS. Even if it did fire on the underlying half, it's a NOTE not a REJECTION.
  - Procedures (`Monitor the information system to detect attacks...`) does NOT name a CSP or commercial product so Rule 8b doesn't short-circuit pre-LLM. Procedures does not contain `auto-compliant per` or any 8a marker either.

Mutation differentiation -- without rule #1 (e.g. deletion or reordering below rule #3):
  - merge([AMBIGUOUS, AFFIRMING]) would fall through to rule #2 (no GAP in list), then rule #3 (AFFIRMING present) -> returns COMPLIANCE_AFFIRMING.
  - _expected_status_for_class(COMPLIANCE_AFFIRMING) = COMPLIANT.
  - Proposed COMPLIANT matches expected COMPLIANT -> no STATUS_NARRATIVE_MISMATCH rejection.
  - LLM proposal accepted on first try -> status=COMPLIANT, source='llm', llm_calls=1, needs_review=False.
  - This case's expected (status=None, source_in=['abstain'], llm_calls=3, needs_review=True) fails loudly -> mutation caught.

Three identical proposals (not three variants): the stub does not consume corrective_context, so the orchestrator's job is just to keep retrying until the budget is gone. Same retry-exhaustion contract as the sibling hybrid and cross-status mismatch cases.

Control SI-4.1 / CCI-001253 (Information System Monitoring) substrate is realistic: the program team has a monitoring plan (USD00088888) that's been examined, but the on-prem section has open POA&M items for missing remediation on legacy servers -- a half that's simultaneously affirming-language AND gap-language. The cloud half is cleanly affirming (separate baseline document). An LLM that retrieved both and saw the affirming framing on each could reasonably propose Compliant; the AMBIGUOUS merge correctly refuses to accept that verdict until the on-prem narrative is rewritten to be internally consistent.

ccis_row.guidance: The organization monitors the information system to detect attacks and indicators of potential attacks in accordance with organization-defined monitoring objectives.
ccis_row.procedures: Monitor the information system to detect attacks and indicators of potential attacks.

tagged_evidence:
## Tagged evidence
- USD00088888 Example System Information System Monitoring Plan Rev B — describes the on-prem monitoring baseline, audit log collection, and the standing POA&M tracking legacy servers awaiting remediation.
- Cloud Monitoring Baseline section 4 — documents audit telemetry collection and forwarding for the cloud half of the boundary.

llm_stub_proposals.narrative:
On-prem: examined USD00088888 monitoring procedures and confirmed via section 2 that audit logs are documented in the plan; however, POA&M opened for missing remediation evidence on three legacy servers.
Cloud: examined the cloud monitoring baseline and confirmed via section 4 that audit telemetry is documented in the SSP.

On-prem: examined USD00088888 monitoring procedures and confirmed via section 2 that audit logs are documented in the plan; however, POA&M opened for missing remediation evidence on three legacy servers.
Cloud: examined the cloud monitoring baseline and confirmed via section 4 that audit telemetry is documented in the SSP.
